minimal_context: The student is sitting at their desk in a regular classroom attempting to complete a written assignment in their book.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please stay in your seat and finish your written work in your book.

STUDENT: (Fidgeting with his stationery items and constantly shifting in his place) I am doing it.

TUTOR: You need to keep writing. You are not keeping pace with the other students.

STUDENT: (Has a dreamy, glazed look for a moment, then suddenly scribbles very fast) Done! I finished it!

TUTOR: This work is incomplete. You need to sit back down and finish the rest of the page.

STUDENT: (Pouts and jumps out of his chair like pop-corn) I don't like written work! (Starts wandering around the classroom making inappropriate noises)

options.A: Provide a structured responsibility that allows for movement, such as having the student collect the books from all the rows.
options.B: Use strategic ignoring by showing a lack of reaction to the inappropriate noises, specifically not acknowledging the wandering behavior.
options.C: Respond with enthusiastic praise, saying, "Another page already? No way! I'm so proud of you! Good job, keep going."
options.D: Pat the student on the shoulder, smile, and ask him to complete another page of the written work.
